column1: # Test: closed-loop steering response (through DBW)
# Format: time_ms
#   - Backwards compatible with the existing Stage 2 CSV format.
#   - `# assert t=<ms>: <expr>` lines are evaluated by tools/test_runner.py
#     against the SensorHub LOG telemetry stream.
# Wire path under test:
#   PC -> SensorHub USB (CMD
#   SensorHub publishes 0x350 NavDrive + 0x100 NavStatus on CAN
#   DBW PID reads 0x350 -> drives L_TURN / R_TURN pins
#   Router observes pins -> integrates angle_tenths
#   Router publishes 0x430 SimSteerActual -> DBW reads -> emits 0x400
#   SensorHub captures 0x400 int16[2] as actual_angle_tenths -> LOG line
# Slew expectation:
#   Router: ANGLE_CHANGE_TENTHS=20 per 100ms loop  =>  ~200 deg*10 / sec
#   Full-lock-to-full-lock (~420 deg*10): ~2.1s of pure Router slew
#   + DBW PID latency + CAN propagation -> samples at least 800ms after
#   the last commanded transition to give the loop time to settle.
# Cruise straight; baseline check.
# assert t=900: -20 <= actual_angle_tenths <= 20
# Command full right (+25 deg = 250 tenths). Slew is 200 deg*10/sec
# assert t=2800: 200 <= actual_angle_tenths <= 250
# Command full left (-25 deg). Full-lock-to-full-lock = 500/200 = 2.5 s.
# assert t=6700: -250 <= actual_angle_tenths <= -200
# Return to center. 250 -> 0 takes 1.25 s.
# assert t=9800: -20 <= actual_angle_tenths <= 20
